title: Grounding-Aware Token Pruning: Recovering from Drastic Performance Drops in Visual Grounding Caused by Pruning

method.name: GAP (Grounding-Aware Token Pruning)
method.purpose: Preserves the grounding ability of Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs)
method.problem_addressed: Token pruning degrades grounding ability, impairing spatial relationships and bounding box predictions
method.root_cause: Misaligned position IDs are the primary cause of performance drops after pruning
method.solution: A simple modification that corrects position ID misalignments

results.performance: GAP consistently improves performance while preserving efficiency
results.applicability: Demonstrated as a viable enhancement for grounding MLLMs
results.validation: Applied to five models and six pruning methods

contributions: Identified misaligned position IDs as the key factor behind grounding performance degradation
Proposed GAP to effectively recover lost performance
Validated GAP across five models and six pruning methods, demonstrating broad applicability

background.MLLM_capabilities: Impressive performance across tasks like VQA and image captioning
background.REC_performance: Models like MiniGPTv2 and Shikra achieve results comparable to specialized models
background.computational_challenge: High computational costs and inefficiencies with increasing visual tokens
background.existing_solutions: Various token pruning methods have been developed to address these issues
